items.note: The polite force-field. Staff will smile and give you space.
Called out to every customer. No reply needed — a nod is plenty.

items.jp: おみやげを さがしています
items.note: 'X o sagashite imasu' aims staff at exactly what you want.

items.jp: みても いいですか
items.note: Ask before handling delicate goods; the answer is almost always yes.

items.jp: これは いくらですか
items.note: Point and ask when the price tag is playing hide and seek.

items.jp: べつのは ありますか
items.en: do you have a different one?
items.note: For another colour, size, or design without naming it.

items.jp: ちょっと ちいさいです
items.note: chotto softens the complaint into a polite hint.

items.jp: ちょっと おおきいです

items.note: Japanese sizes run small; an M often fits like a UK S.

items.jp: しちゃく できますか
items.note: Shoes off before a fitting room; they may hand you a face cover.

items.jp: ふくろ おねがいします
items.note: Bags often cost a few yen now, so they ask. Say this if you want one.

items.jp: ふくろは いりません
items.note: The eco answer. A small hand-wave helps the message land.
The konbini asks if you need them with hot food; say hai or irimasen.
Polite refusal for anything offered — spoons, bags, receipts.
The konbini bentō question. Answer with onegaishimasu or sono mama de.

items.jp: あたためて ください
そのままで だいじょうぶです
items.note: The 'no need to heat it' reply. Also useful for 'leave it as is'.
'reji wa dochira desu ka' — where's the register — in a big store.
They'll ask 'pointo kādo wa?' — just say irimasen unless you have one.
Boxed regional sweets to share back home — a whole beautiful genre.
Signs say 'Tax-Free'. Threshold is usually ¥5,000+ in one store, same day.

items.jp: めんぜいは できますか
items.note: Ask at the till with your passport ready.

items.jp: つつんで もらえますか
items.note: Wrapping is an art here and often free. Worth asking for gifts.
Tags show zeikomi (incl.) and zeinuki (excl.). Check which you're reading.

lessons.title: A bit big, a bit small

dialogues.scene: Don Quijote, midnight, five floors of everything. You've grabbed snacks and a gift; the till beeps and the clerk fires off the ritual questions.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.jp: いらっしゃいませ！ポイントカードは ありますか？
turns.romaji: irasshaimase! pointo kādo wa arimasu ka?
turns.en: welcome! do you have a point card?

turns.speaker: you
turns.stakes: You don't. Decline without slowing the queue.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.jp: おべんとうは あたためますか？

turns.speaker: you
turns.stakes: It's a cold night and you're starving. Ask him to heat it.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.jp: こちらの おみやげ、めんぜいに できますよ。
turns.romaji: kochira no omiyage, menzei ni dekimasu yo.
turns.en: this gift can be tax-free, by the way.

turns.speaker: you
turns.stakes: Worth the saving. Confirm you'd like tax-free.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.jp: パスポートを おねがいします。ふくろは？
turns.en: your passport, please. and a bag?

turns.speaker: you
turns.stakes: Your arms are full. Yes — ask for a bag.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.jp: ぜんぶで さんぜんえんです。

turns.speaker: you
turns.stakes: Cash is low after the izakaya. Ask if card's OK.
